name: brain-offline-fallback
description: Brain class implements 3-tier offline fallback: Full (BM25+vec+GraphRAG), Degraded (BM25+GraphRAG), Minimal (BM25-only). _try_enable_vector() loads sqlite-vec extension then model2vec; self.vector_enabled gates all vec paths.

name: rrf-fusion-pattern
description: reciprocal_rank_fusion() fuses N result lists: score[doc_id] += 1/(k+rank) for each list. Docs appearing in multiple lists get boosted scores. k=60 default. Always include BM25 list; add vector/graph lists when available.
brain_bootstrap() only indexed docs/ and core-steps/; if neither existed sources=[] and Brain was empty
resolution: Always add common src dirs (src/lib/scripts/plugins/hooks) and cwd fallback; always call ingest()

name: brain-stop-hook-fallback-reindex
description: prism_stop_hook conductor fallback: call Brain().incremental_reindex() before build_agent_instruction() when Conductor unavailable so Brain knowledge stays current

name: brain-corrupt-error-detection
description: Brain.__init__ wraps PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL in try/except sqlite3.DatabaseError → raises BrainCorruptError with db path label. _check_db_integrity() runs PRAGMA integrity_check as secondary guard. brain_bootstrap() catches BrainCorruptError, deletes .prism/brain/*.db, retries Brain() once.

FTS5 external content table (content='docs') requires AFTER INSERT/DELETE/UPDATE triggers on the content table. Without them: (1) INSERT OR REPLACE desynchronizes FTS5 silently, (2) manual DELETE FROM docs_fts WHERE id=? is wrong syntax for content tables.
resolution: Add docs_fts_ai/ad/au triggers in _init_brain_schema() executescript; remove manual FTS5 ops from _ingest_single() and _remove_entries_by_source(). Triggers handle all sync automatically.

name: brain-deps-actionable-messaging
description: _try_enable_vector() uses informative degraded mode message ('running in BM25+GraphRAG mode') instead of alarming 'unavailable'; __main__ init/ingest attempts pip install of sqlite-vec model2vec before Brain(); _cmd_init/_cmd_status print mode label (Full/BM25+GraphRAG).

name: brain-exclude-path-segments
description: Brain._should_index() uses _EXCLUDED_PATH_SEGMENTS set (.claude, .prism, __pycache__, node_modules, .git, .venv, venv, .env, dist, build, .tox, .mypy_cache, .pytest_cache, .overstory) with Path.parts membership check to skip state/cache dirs before suffix check.

name: brain-passive-vs-active-usage
description: Issue #8: Brain context appears passively injected but not actively queried. Root causes already fixed: path exclusions (3.0.6) prevent indexing state files, gate blocking (3.0.7) ensures workflow stops properly. In fresh/simple projects, Brain naturally has minimal useful content — this is expected.

name: brain-purge-deleted-cleanup
description: Brain _purge_deleted() method: queries all docs with source_file, removes entries where file no longer exists or fails _should_index(). Called at end of ingest() and incremental_reindex(). incremental_reindex uses --diff-filter=ACMRD (added D) and separately handles deleted files.

name: brain-relevance-threshold
description: Brain.system_context() filters search results by rrf_score >= 0.1 threshold before including in context. Results below threshold are excluded. last_result_count reflects post-filter count.
Brain relevance threshold 0.1 was mathematically unreachable with RRF k=60. Max possible: 2/61=0.033 (2 lists) or 3/61=0.049 (3 lists). Caused bq=0 on all steps despite Brain being active.
resolution: Lowered threshold to 0.02. Always validate thresholds against the scoring function output range.

name: vector-domain-filter
description: sqlite-vec vec0 domain filter: _vector_search() cannot use WHERE on non-vec columns. Fix: over-fetch limit*3 when domain set, then post-filter by joining doc_id to docs table (SELECT id FROM docs WHERE id IN (...) AND domain = ?). Cap final results at original limit.

name: sqlite-conn-execute-readonly
description: sqlite3.Connection.execute is read-only in Python — cannot be monkeypatched directly. To intercept SQL calls in tests, replace the entire connection attribute (e.g. brain._brain) with a wrapper class that has its own execute() method delegating to the real connection for non-intercepted queries.

name: auto-bootstrap-search
description: Brain.search() auto-bootstraps: if docs table has 0 rows, calls ingest(_cli_source_dirs()) before searching. Also calls incremental_reindex() on non-empty searches (may need throttling).

name: mulch-expertise-ingestion
description: Brain._ingest_mulch_expertise() reads .mulch/expertise/*.jsonl, indexes each record as domain=expertise with doc_id=expertise:{domain_name}:{record_id}. Called from ingest().

name: vector-domain-post-filter
description: Brain._vector_search() post-filters domain: over-fetches 3x, joins doc_id back to docs table for domain check, returns filtered[:limit]. sqlite-vec vec0 doesn't support WHERE on non-vec columns.

name: brain-cli-new-commands
description: _cmd_graph(brain, entity) prints entity relationships from brain.graph_query(); _cmd_explain(brain, file) shows indexed chunks and graph entities via direct DB queries; _cmd_rebuild(brain) calls _purge_deleted() then ingest(). All dispatched in __main__.

name: overstory-logs-source-file-none
description: _ingest_overstory_logs() uses source_file=None (not the real path) because .overstory is in _EXCLUDED_PATH_SEGMENTS — passing the real path causes _purge_deleted() to immediately remove the just-indexed entries. Uses doc_id='sessions:<rel_path>' for content-hash deduplication.

name: brain-analytics-cmd
description: _cmd_analytics() reads outcomes.jsonl from .prism/brain/ directory, groups by (persona, step_id), shows avg/best/worst scores and last 10 recent outcomes. Dispatch via 'analytics' command in brain_engine.py __main__ block.
